perturbation.original_text: Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Confidential Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed. Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within twenty (20) calendar days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE").
perturbation.changed_text: Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Confidential Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed. Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within five (5) business days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE").
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the timeframe for returning or destroying confidential materials upon receiving a Termination Notice. Initially, the Recipient has twenty (20) calendar days. The modification changes this period to five (5) business days while the text still refers to the original twenty (20) period, creating ambiguity regarding the actual compliance deadline. This directly impacts the enforcement of the return/destruction obligations, as it's unclear which deadline should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Confidential Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed. Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within five (5) business days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE").
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the timeframe for compliance from twenty (20) calendar days to five (5) business days, but it retains the original reference to the "foregoing twenty (20) period" for the compliance certification. This creates a contradiction because the Recipient is given two different deadlines within the same section. The changed text contradicts itself regarding the deadline to comply with the notice, creating an internal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will continue to be bound by its obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will ONLY be bound by its obligations under this Agreement for 30 days with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.explanation: The modification creates an in-text contradiction by limiting the duration of the Recipient's confidentiality obligations following the return or destruction of Confidential Materials. The original text states that the obligations continue indefinitely. The change imposes a 30-day limit, creating uncertainty and conflict in Section 5. The original text implies continued obligation while the changed text limits that same obligation to a 30-day period. This directly contradicts the extent of obligations enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the -2- <PAGE> provisions of this Section 5, if Recipient receives a Termination Notice, it shall not be required to return or destroy Confidential Materials to the extent that, in the reasonable opinion of legal counsel for such party, such return or destruction violates any law or regulation; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event Recipient shall describe in its Compliance Certificate the extent to which it is not returning or destroying Confidential Materials. Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will ONLY be bound by its obligations under this Agreement for 30 days with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.justification: The changed text imposes a 30-day limit on confidentiality obligations after the return or destruction of Confidential Materials, directly contradicting the original text's implication of continued obligation. The contradicted text incorporates the changed text. The contradictory change introduces an inconsistency, as it limits the duration of the confidentiality, thereby contradicting the clause's original intent and creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any term or provision of this Agreement may be amended only by the written consent of each of the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: Any term or provision of this Agreement may be amended only by the written consent of Premisys.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction in Section 14. Initially, amendments require written consent from BOTH parties. The change dictates that amendments can be made solely with the written consent of Premisys. This undermines the mutual agreement required, causing imbalance. The original indicates mutual obligation, the change only requires one party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any term or provision of this Agreement may be amended only by the written consent of Premisys. The observance of any term of this Agreement may be waived (either generally or in a particular instance and either retroactively or prospectively) only by a writing signed by the party to be bound by such waiver. The waiver by a party of any breach hereof or default in the performance hereof will not be deemed to constitute a waiver of any other default or any succeeding breach or default.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement, stating that only Premisys' consent is required for amendments, whereas the contradicted text states the same thing. However, the original text prior to the change stated that both parties' consent was needed. Therefore, the contradicted text and changed text are the same, but contradict the original intent, and thus still presents a clear contradiction given the explanation, especially when compared to the original text.
